00029 
00030 #include <unistd.h>
00031 #include <err.h>
00032 
00033 /*
00034  * mv - move (rename) files.
00035  * Usage: mv oldfile newfile
00036  *
00037  * Just calls rename() on them. If it fails, we don't attempt to
00038  * figure out which filename was wrong or what happened.
00039  *
00040  * In certain circumstances, Unix mv will fall back to copying and
00041  * deleting the old copy. We don't do that.
00042  *
00043  * We also don't allow the Unix form of
00044  *     mv file1 file2 file3 destination-dir
00045  */
00046 
00047 static
00048 void
00049 dorename(const char *oldfile, const char *newfile)
00050 {
00051         if (rename(oldfile, newfile)) {
00052                 err(1, "%s or %s", oldfile, newfile);
00053         }
00054 }
00055 
00056 int
00057 main(int argc, char *argv[])
00058 {
00059         if (argc!=3) {
00060                 errx(1, "Usage: mv oldfile newfile");
00061         }
00062         dorename(argv[1], argv[2]);
00063         return 0;
00064 }
